meta.title: X761 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Jaguar F-Pace X761 engine — 2.0 Ingenium diesel & petrol, 3.0 V6, 5.0 V8 SVR. Real UK costs, failure data, and honest reliability advice. Get free quotes from 15+ vetted Jaguar specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar F-Pace X761 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X761 engine typically costs £3,000–£14,000 supply only, depending on engine — the 2.0-litre petrol sits at £3,000–£5,000, while the 5.0 V8 SVR can reach £9,000–£14,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar F-Pace X761 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 3.0-litre supercharged V6 (AJ126) is generally considered the most characterful and, when maintained, a dependable X761 engine — but it has a known coolant Y-pipe weakness that must be addressed proactively. The 2.0-litre petrol (PT204) is also a solid, simpler choice.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar F-Pace X761?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch on the 2.0-litre diesel (204DTD/AJ200D), coolant Y-pipe failure on the 3.0-litre supercharged V6 (AJ126), water pump failure on the 2.0-litre petrol (PT204), and supercharger isolator wear on the 5.0 V8 SVR (508PS) — plus the usual JLR electrical gremlins.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar F-Pace X761 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Reliability varies significantly by engine choice. The 2.0-litre diesel has a well-documented timing chain risk, particularly on pre-2019 cars. The 3.0 V6 and 2.0 petrol are generally more dependable but have known cooling system weaknesses. A well-maintained example can be reliable; a neglected one will be expensive.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar F-Pace X761 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 2.0-litre diesel (D165/D180/D200) without documented timing chain history — particularly pre-2019 units. A 5.0 V8 SVR without meticulous service records is also a risky purchase. No engine is inherently bad, but history is everything.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F-Pace X761 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle (2.0 diesel timing chain), overheating or coolant loss with no visible leak (3.0 V6 Y-pipe), or persistent check engine light and rough running are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar F-Pace X761 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a £15,000 F-Pace, a £4,500 diesel recon is 30% — safe. Against a tired £10,000 example, the same recon is 45% — borderline but still within our 60% Jaguar threshold. On a well-kept F-Pace, replacement almost always makes sense.

mainEntity.name: How long do Jaguar F-Pace X761 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 3.0 V6 and 2.0 petrol can exceed 150,000–200,000 miles with proper maintenance. The 2.0 diesel's limiting factor is the timing chain — addressed proactively, it can also reach high mileage. The 5.0 V8 is durable but requires specialist maintenance for the supercharger.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F-Pace X761 and the E-Pace X540?

acceptedAnswer.text: The X761 is the first-generation F-Pace (2015–2025) — a mid-size SUV on the D7a platform. The X540 is the E-Pace (2017–present) — a smaller compact SUV on a different platform. The X761 has a wider engine range including V6 and V8 options; the X540 is four-cylinder only.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar F-Pace X761 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible on the engine itself. Tell us your variant (D180, P250, P380, SVR) and approximate registration date and we'll identify it — 15+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Jaguar F-Pace X761 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the Jaguar F-Pace X761 — from the Ingenium four-cylinder diesels to the 5.0-litre supercharged V8 SVR. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and the Ingenium timing chain risk that defines this generation. Backed by 220+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Jaguar F-Pace X761 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F-Pace — from the 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium units to the 3.0-litre supercharged V6 and the 5.0-litre supercharged V8 SVR.

overview.intro: The X761 is the first SUV ever produced by Jaguar, launched in 2015 and entering production in February 2016. Built on the JLR D7a aluminium platform, it introduced Jaguar to the luxury crossover segment with a range of Ingenium four-cylinder engines, a supercharged V6, and later a 5.0-litre supercharged V8 SVR flagship.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: JLR D7a aluminium architecture • Predecessor: None — first Jaguar SUV • Successor: Current model — production ended December 2025 • Sibling body styles in this generation: 5-door SUV only
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The X761 F-Pace generated 220 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel as these vehicles exit warranty and face timing chain issues.

items.quote: The 3.0-litre V6 diesel delivers genuine six-cylinder refinement and 300bhp without the four-cylinder Ingenium's timing chain anxiety. It's the diesel to buy if you can stomach the fuel economy.
items.whoItsFor: Buyers who need diesel torque without the four-cylinder's risk profile.

items.quote: The supercharged V6 from the F-Type delivers 380bhp with linear, responsive power — but the plastic coolant Y-pipe is a known weakness on early cars.
items.whoItsFor: Enthusiasts who want Jaguar character without the V8's running costs.

items.quote: The 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel has a rear-mounted timing chain that stretches and can fail catastrophically — cold-start rattle is the warning.
items.whoItsFor: Only buyers with documented chain replacement history.

items.quote: The supercharged AJ133 V8 delivers 550bhp but when it fails — typically timing chain tensioner wear or supercharger isolator failure — repairs require specialist intervention and five-figure bills.
items.whoItsFor: Buyers who can absorb £20k+ repair bills.

items.quote: The 2.0-litre petrol Ingenium avoids diesel emissions complexity entirely and delivers 250bhp from a 2.0-litre. Post-2019 cars have updated timing chains and fuel pumps.
items.whoItsFor: Buyers who want petrol reliability without the V6's purchase premium.

items.quote: The 300bhp version of the 2.0-litre petrol Ingenium offers genuine performance without the supercharged V6's cooling system vulnerabilities.
items.whoItsFor: Buyers who want the most powerful four-cylinder option without stepping up to V6 running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Risk vs Reward — post-2019 cars safer
Best Diesel — six-cylinder refinement without chain anxiety
Solid Choice — simpler than diesels, fewer emissions components
Best Petrol — characterful, but cooling system needs monitoring
Most Expensive — outstanding performance, outstanding running costs

economicsRule.title: The X761 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,500–£6,000 2.0 diesel recon against a £15,000 F-Pace sits at 23–40% of the car's value — within our 60% Jaguar threshold. Against a £30,000 example, the same recon drops to 12–20% — a routine repair decision. Against a £10,000 high-mileage example, the same recon at 35–60% is borderline. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X761 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 2.0 P250 petrol — no DPF, no AdBlue, simpler emissions system.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X761 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 508PS 5.0 V8 SVR — supercharger and timing chain repairs are specialist-only.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X761 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: AJ126 3.0 P380 — V6 character without the V8's purchase or running costs.

problems.title: 2.0 Ingenium Diesel Timing Chain Stretch
problems.rootCause: The 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel's timing chain is located at the rear of the engine, making it a major labour-intensive job to replace. The chain, guides, and tensioners can wear prematurely, causing chain stretch. If the chain stretches too far, it can "jump" a tooth or snap, causing pistons to strike open valves in this interference engine. Early chains (2015–2017) were made of an inferior material that has since been updated.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold-start rattle on a 2.0-litre diesel F-Pace, get the timing system inspected immediately. An £1,200–£3,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,500–£6,000 replacement that follows if ignored. Avoid pre-2019 models without full recall compliance.

cta.label: Compare 204DTD engine prices for your F-Pace X761 →

problems.title: AJ126 3.0 V6 Supercharged Coolant Y-Pipe Failure
problems.rootCause: The plastic coolant crossover or "Y-pipe" is a regular culprit in catastrophic cooling failures on the 3.0-litre supercharged V6. When this pipe cracks or fails, coolant is lost rapidly, leading to overheating and potential engine seizure. Jaguar acknowledged the issue but redesigned the part using the same plastic material.
problems.recommendation: Have the coolant Y-pipe inspected on any 3.0 V6 F-Pace, particularly 2017 models. Consider replacing it proactively as a preventative measure — a £500–£1,000 job is far cheaper than a £4,500+ engine replacement. Monitor the temperature gauge obsessively.

cta.label: Compare AJ126 engine prices for your F-Pace X761 →

problems.title: 508PS 5.0 V8 SVR Supercharger Isolator & Timing Chain
problems.rootCause: The 5.0-litre supercharged V8 uses a supercharger isolator that can wear, causing a rattling noise that varies with RPM. The timing chain tensioner can also wear on earlier units. The F-Pace SVR has a smaller oil pan than Range Rover products, and some have been known not to make it to the 16,000-mile oil change interval without being down several quarts.
problems.recommendation: If you own an SVR, be early on maintenance — shorten oil change intervals to 8,000–10,000 miles and consider oil analysis. Listen for supercharger rattle and have it investigated immediately.

cta.label: Compare 508PS engine prices for your F-Pace X761 SVR →

problems.title: PT204 2.0 Ingenium Petrol Water Pump & Cooling System
problems.rootCause: The 2.0-litre petrol Ingenium's water pump can fail, leading to coolant loss and overheating. The cooling system is generally less problematic than the diesel version, but water pump failure is a known pattern.
problems.recommendation: Replace the water pump proactively at 60,000 miles on any 2.0-litre petrol F-Pace. Monitor the temperature gauge and investigate any coolant loss immediately.

cta.label: Compare PT204 engine prices for your F-Pace X761 →
